intro: Gyrostachys bicolor, also known as the two-colored club moss, is a type of gymnosperm plant that belongs to the family Podocarpaceae. Traditionally, Gyrostachys bicolor has been used in folk medicine, exhibiting potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can aid in wound healing, reducing fever, and protecting against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Gyrostachys bicolor include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Gyrostachys bicolor is often prepared into teas, infusions, and decoctions, and is also used in the production of essential oils and extracts for various therapeutic applications.
